body.title: Audit check: flag devices missing structured trace emission
body.result: AR-008 check registered; granny/device.py fixed with trace_record at 4 decision points; audit_check_missing_traces.py committed
body.description:
The /diagnose skill fell back to log-grepping Granny because no structured traces existed in datacenter_logs/granny-weatherwax/trace/. Every rack device should emit structured JSONL trace events via DiagnosticBase so /diagnose works uniformly. Add a forever audit check that detects devices inheriting BaseDevice but NOT emitting traces (no DiagnosticBase inheritance, or no trace_record() calls in device code).

Register via audit_add.py as a shell/python kind check. On each audit sweep: find all classes in devices/ that inherit BaseDevice, verify each has DiagnosticBase in its MRO or calls trace_record(), flag any that don't. Severity: MED (traces are observability infrastructure, not blocking).

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/audit_add.py (register the check), lab/claudecode/audit_runner.py (runs it), devices/granny/device.py is the immediate offender to fix after check is registered
**Design rules:** audit checks are registered forever unless explicitly removed; MED severity = ticket if not trivial
**Scope boundary:** IN — register the audit check; fix granny/device.py to inherit DiagnosticBase as the first example; OUT — fixing all other devices (each gets its own pass)
**Completion criteria:** `python3 lab/claudecode/audit_runner.py --drain` flags devices missing trace emission; granny/device.py no longer flagged (DiagnosticBase added); check persists across future audit runs
